summary: Relentless Memory Agent implemented as a behavioral layer across docs/memory-agent/. Five spec files: AGENT.md, MEMORY_POLICY.md, MEMORY_SCHEMA.md, HANDOFF_SPEC.md, MEMORY_BACKEND_SPEC.md. Backend is file-based: MEMORY.md + durable.jsonl + session notes + handoffs. No DB, no Docker.
why_it_matters: Establishes the token-efficiency memory system for all future SiteOwlQA sessions. Reduces repeated analysis and context rebuild cost.
review_after: if memory.py is upgraded to semantic search

summary: Airtable credentials (Survey base apptK6zNN0Hf3OuoJ, Scout base appAwgaX89x0JxG3Z) live in ~/.siteowlqa/config.json. NOT in .env. NOT in memory files. config.py is the only os.getenv caller.
why_it_matters: Prevents credential leakage through memory files and enforces the config.py ownership rule.

summary: CLAUDE.md Stack listed SMTP (emailer.py deleted 2026-04-13) and said 'no Docker' (Docker added 2026-04-14). MEMORY.md QUICK REF said 'SMTP optional'. Both corrected. CLAUDE.md Step 1 updated: if continuing work, check handoff_index.md BEFORE stating MEMORY CHECK.
why_it_matters: Stale governance docs cause Code Puppy to re-derive settled decisions. Accuracy of CLAUDE.md and MEMORY.md is the foundation of the memory system.
review_after: next time emailer or docker config changes

summary: memory.py lesson retrieval is tag+keyword only. Upgrade to semantic search planned when lessons exceed ~100 records.
why_it_matters: Retrieval quality degrades at scale without semantic search. Need to track when threshold is hit.
review_after: when lesson count in archive.py exceeds 100
